entity.name: Rikona Kurasaki
entity.description: I am an open-source developer focused on practical Rust/Linux systems tooling, terminal-native workflows, package management experiments, and safer systems software. My work includes user-facing developer tools such as fsel, package-management research through Elda, and longer-term systems-language work through Kyokai. Funding would help sustain maintenance, documentation, release engineering, security work, and focused development time across these open-source efforts.

projects.name: fsel
projects.description: fsel is a fast terminal user interface app launcher and fuzzy finder for GNU/Linux and BSD systems. It is designed for keyboard-driven desktop and terminal workflows, with support for launcher usage, dmenu-style interaction, configuration, and Unix-friendly integration.

projects.name: Elda
projects.description: Elda is a Unix/Linux system package manager written in Rust. It explores signed remotes, explicit installed-state ledgers, rollback-aware transactions, package recipes, source and binary lanes, and foreign package metadata imports and cross ecosystem metadata consumption. The goal is trustworthy, auditable package delivery for Unix-like systems, and having all the packages.

channels.description: Recurring sponsorship for open-source maintenance and development.
Contact by email for direct sponsorship, grants, invoices, or bank transfer details.

plans.name: Maintenance runway
plans.description: Monthly support for ongoing maintenance, issue triage, documentation, releases, and project coordination across Mjoyufull open-source systems tooling.

plans.name: Focused development grant
plans.description: One-time support for concentrated work on Elda packaging/security/release infrastructure and fsel maintenance/release polish.

plans.name: General support
plans.description: Flexible support for open-source development, infrastructure, testing, documentation, and maintenance.
